content.full_text:
[Seal of the State of California]     California
                                               Fair Political
                                               Practices Commission


                                               June 26, 1991


Elizabeth Ford
Deputy Clerk
Office of the Clerk-Recorder
221 S. Mooney Blvd,
Courthouse Room G28
Visalia, Ca 93291

                                               Re: Your Request for
                                                   Informal Assistance
                                                   Our File No. I-91-296

Dear Ms. Ford:

     You have requested advice concerning the campaign provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/  Because your question
is general in nature, we are treating your request as one for
informal assistance.2/

                                QUESTION

     What provision of the Act requires officeholders receiving a
salary of at least $100 a month to file campaign statements?

                                DISCUSSION

     Section 84200(a) requires elected officers, candidates, and
committees to file semiannual campaign statements each year no
later than July 31 for the period ending June 30, and no later
than January 31 for the period ending December 31.  However,
elected officers whose salaries are less than $100 per month,
judges, judicial candidates, and their controlled committees are

_________

1/     Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/     Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections
18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 •(916) 322-5660

page 2
Ford, Elizabeth


not required to file semiannual statements for any six-month
period in which they have not made or received any contributions
or made any expenditures. (Section 84200(a)(2).)

Candidates or officeholders who plan to receive contributions
totaling less than $1,000 and who plan to make expenditures
totaling less than $1,000 in a calendar year may file one short
form campaign statement (Form 470) covering the entire calendar
year. During a year when an elected officer is not being voted
upon, the filing deadline for Form 470 is normally July 31. No
other campaign statements are required to be filed by a candidate
or officeholder who has filed the short form campaign statement
for activity in the calendar year unless the candidate or
officeholder subsequently receives $1,000 or more in contributions
or makes $1,000 or more in expenditures. (Regulation 18406.)

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections 18329(c)(3).)

not required to file semiannual statements for any six-month period in which they have not made or received any contributions or made any expenditures. (Section 84200(a)(2).)

During a year when an elected officer is not being voted upon, the filing deadline for Form 470 is normally July 31. No other campaign statements are required to be filed by a candidate or officeholder who has filed the short form campaign statement for activity in the calendar year unless the candidate or officeholder subsequently receives $1,000 or more in contributions or makes $1,000 or more in expenditures. (Regulation 18406.)
